claims: The skill's stated purpose is to "analyze liquidity pool metrics," yet it requests excessive capabilities: 1) It requires API keys for exchanges, which could grant access to user trading accounts and funds — unnecessary for read-only pool analysis. 2) It includes generating "trading signals" and "opportunity recommendations," which extend beyond analytical reporting into active trading advice. 3) It demands access to "exchange order book and trade history" and "token holder distribution," which are broader than necessary for core TVL/volume/fee analysis. 4) The allowed Write and Edit permissions combined with trading APIs could enable unauthorized modifications or transactions. A liquidity pool analysis tool should only need read access to on-chain data and market APIs, not trading capabilities or full exchange account access.
